children.text: Each State shall accept and use the mail voter registration application form prescribed by the Federal Election Commission pursuant to for the registration of voters in elections for Federal office.section 20508(a)(2) of this title

In addition to accepting and using the form described in paragraph (1), a State may develop and use a mail voter registration form that meets all of the criteria stated in for the registration of voters in elections for Federal office.section 20508(b) of this title
A form described in paragraph (1) or (2) shall be accepted and used for notification of a registrant’s change of address.

children.heading: Availability of forms
children.label: b Availability of forms
children.text: The chief State election official of a State shall make the forms described in subsection (a) available for distribution through governmental and private entities, with particular emphasis on making them available for organized voter registration programs.

children.label: A
children.text: the person was registered to vote in a jurisdiction by mail; and

children.label: B
children.text: the person has not previously voted in that jurisdiction.

children.label: A
children.text: who is entitled to vote by absentee ballot under the Uniformed and Overseas Citizens Absentee Voting Act [ et seq.];52 U.S.C. 20301

children.label: B
children.text: who is provided the right to vote otherwise than in person under ; orsection 20102(b)(2)(B)(ii) of this title

children.label: C
children.text: who is entitled to vote otherwise than in person under any other Federal law.

children.heading: Undelivered notices
children.label: d Undelivered notices
children.text: If a notice of the disposition of a mail voter registration application under is sent by nonforwardable mail and is returned undelivered, the registrar may proceed in accordance with .section 20507(a)(2) of this titlesection 20507(d) of this title
